mnemonics.title: RISC Key Characteristics
mnemonics.content: LISF-LR: Load-Store, Fixed-length, Large registers, Single cycle, Simple addressing
IF-ID-EX-MEM-WB: Imagine Folding Executable Memory While Building
mnemonics.title: Pipeline Stages Mnemonic

acronyms.usage: CPU design philosophy with simple, fast instructions
Traditional CPU design with complex, multi-cycle instructions
Hardware component performing arithmetic and logical operations
Register holding address of next instruction to fetch

visualTips.visualization: Draw a two-column table: RISC (left) with simple icons for fixed length, register operations, single cycle; CISC (right) with complex icons for variable length, memory operations, multi-cycle
Draw horizontal timeline with 5 rows (IF, ID, EX, MEM, WB). Show 3-4 instructions staggered horizontally to visualize parallel execution and pipeline stages

Draw CPU core as central box, registers as small boxes inside, memory as large box outside. Show arrows: LOAD (memory to register), STORE (register to memory), ALU operations (register to register only)
Show 32-bit fixed boxes for RISC vs variable boxes (8, 16, 24, 32 bits) for CISC to demonstrate fetch simplicity in RISC

analogies.concept: RISC as a Factory Assembly Line
analogies.analogy: Just as an assembly line has simple, specialized stations each performing one task efficiently, RISC has simple instructions each doing one task in one cycle. Complex jobs require many simple steps.
CISC is like a restaurant with complex pre-made dishes (complex instructions); RISC is like cooking with fresh ingredients where each simple step (instruction) is executed to create the final dish.

Think of registers as your immediate workspace on a desk, while memory is a storage room across the building. RISC keeps data in registers (desk) for quick access rather than running to memory (storage) frequently.

Pipeline is like car assembly: while one car is being painted (EX), another is being assembled (ID), and another is having parts fetched (IF) - all happening simultaneously for different cars (instructions).
